Title: The mitochondrial NAD+ transporter (NDT1) plays important roles in cellular NAD+ homeostasis in Arabidopsis thaliana

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitously expressed; strongest in pollen grain; also considerable in seeds, seedlings, mature leaves and flowers; highly expressed during later stages of pollen development and following germination; also expressed in guard cells
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongest in pollen grain; also in seeds, seedlings, mature leaves and flowers
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cellular pyridine nucleotide pools including leaves, siliques, seeds/pollen-associated tissues, mitochondria
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential redox coenzyme supporting cellular metabolism, photosynthesis-linked redox balance, pollen viability, seed development, germination and lipid mobilization
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Maintains cellular NAD+ homeostasis; reduced AtNDT1 lowers NAD+ levels, increases NADH/NAD+ ratio, alters NADP(H) pools, enhances leaf photosynthesis and starch/sucrose accumulation, but impairs pollen viability, pollen tube growth, silique development, seed set, germination and seedling establishment
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA insertion mutant; antisense repression under 35S promoter; qRT-PCR; stable GFP localization; metabolite profiling; nucleotide quantification; fatty-acid profiling; pollen viability/germination assays; reciprocal crosses; photosynthesis measurements
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter (antisense repression lines); ubiquitin10 promoter (GFP localization constructs)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Short-day growth conditions; light/dark diurnal cycle; high light intensities for NPQ analyses; seed imbibition/germination
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating mitochondrial NAD+ transport can reprogram redox and carbon metabolism, but reduced AtNDT1 causes major reproductive penalties; useful for understanding cofactor homeostasis rather than direct crop improvement unless balanced tissue-specific engineering is used
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe reproductive defects including lower pollen viability, reduced pollen tube growth, shorter siliques, fewer seeds, higher seed abortion, slower germination and impaired seedling establishment despite enhanced vegetative growth
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNDT1 is a mitochondrial NAD+ transporter highly expressed in pollen. 2. Downregulation decreases NAD+ and shifts cellular redox state toward a more reduced condition. 3. Reduced AtNDT1 enhances vegetative growth, photosynthesis, starch and sucrose accumulation. 4. Reduced AtNDT1 severely impairs pollen function, seed set, germination and storage reserve mobilization.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously presumed chloroplastic, but this study demonstrates exclusive mitochondrial localization in stably transformed Arabidopsis. Acts as a major determinant of mitochondrial NAD+ import and affects whole-cell pyridine nucleotide rebalancing.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GC-TOF-MS metabolite profiling; GC-TOF-MS fatty-acid profiling; no public accession reported
